header.headline: Transformer Overview
header.subtitle: The block diagram matters less as a picture than as a repeated contract: route, mix, project, normalize, repeat.

left.title: Data path
left.body: Tokens enter, attention mixes context, MLP reshapes representation, and residuals keep the path stable.

right.title: Scaling path
right.body: As layers repeat, systems questions shift from one block to memory, bandwidth, and compiler-visible structure.

panels.title: Attention core
panels.summary: Attention is the routing mechanism: what matters now, and what can be ignored.

panels.title: MLP reshape
panels.summary: Feed-forward layers turn mixed context into a richer feature basis for the next step.
panels.note: Not all intelligence is in attention.

panels.title: Residual stability
panels.summary: Residual paths and normalization make repetition trainable and serving more predictable.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: The transformer is a repeated program, not just a diagram.
That makes it a natural fit for deterministic DSL training.
Learn the contract once, then compose it.
